name: TAO Post Expansion Runner v1
edge_mechanism: post-expansion participation with partial-profit runner management
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing whether a post-expansion entry with partial-profit runner management can outperform flat one-shot exits by harvesting the first push while keeping a small core for persistence. The edge is management structure after valid expansion, not prediction of long trends.
invalidation_condition: Abort if post-expansion participation is too sparse, if the first target rarely gets hit, or if the runner never contributes enough after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A valid expansion has already occurred, price remains structurally aligned with fast value, and a follow-through leg develops with enough amplitude to justify scaling out and leaving a small runner.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In volatile chop, full-trend capture is unreliable, but expansion afterglow often exists for one more leg. Partial-profit plus runner management should monetize that asymmetry better than all-in/all-out logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is suitable because follow-through amplitude is large enough for staged exits to matter.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If expansions mean-revert instantly, partial-profit runner structure will not save the trade because even the first leg will be too weak.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the first target hits but the runner adds no value, reduce or remove the runner before changing the entry logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Selective entries where the first target captures most expectancy and the runner contributes occasionally but meaningfully.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h and one cross-lane show tradable density, decent first-target hit rate, and at least occasional runner contribution after costs.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too few setups, or expansions that never follow through enough for staged exits.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; post-expansion participation and staged exits are compatible
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad post-expansion hold logic rather than extreme breakout rarity

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for testing whether staged exits add value after valid expansion.
validation_basket.continue_if: First target hit rate is acceptable and the runner occasionally contributes after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Setups are too sparse or runner contribution is consistently nonexistent.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: A few meaningful post-expansion entries with front-half harvesting and occasional runner upside.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same management logic has value on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows the same first-target asymmetry, even with smaller amplitude.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The setup only works on TAO volatility.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner post-expansion runner opportunities.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the management edge transfers to another major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC preserves the staged-exit edge without requiring TAO-specific volatility.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More orderly but smaller runner contribution than TAO.
